title: Molten Adventurous Chinese Chicken Pilaf
description: A spicy and flavorful pilaf with a molten center, featuring crispy fried rice and a tangy pico de gallo sauce.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of mustard oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the mixed vegetables, ginger, garlic, cumin, and chili flakes to the skillet and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the cooked Basmati rice, pico de gallo sauce, and salt to the skillet and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes.
Make a well in the center of the skillet and add 1 tablespoon of mustard oil.
Pour the khao khua mixture into the well and cook until the mixture starts to set, about 2 minutes.
Use a spatula to break up the khao khua mixture and cook for another minute.
Use a spoon to scoop up some of the pilaf mixture and place it in a small ramekin or mini cast-iron skillet.
Top the pilaf with some of the khao khua mixture and place the ramekin in the oven.
Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for 5-7 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the filling is hot and bubbly.
Serve the molten pilaf hot, garnished with chopped cilantro or scallions if desired.
